perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Customer" means a person or entity that has acquired, or has indicated its interest in acquiring from Licensee, or from a Redistributor if so specified herein, a non-exclusive and nontransferable Sublicense to use one (1) or more of the Licensed Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Customer" means a person or entity that has acquired, or has indicated its interest in acquiring from Licensee, or from a Redistributor if so specified herein, a non-exclusive and nontransferable Sublicense to use one (1) or more of the Licensed Products, and has maintained an active subscription to support services for a minimum of one year.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a requirement for Customers to maintain an active subscription to support services for at least one year. This contradicts the original definition by adding another condition to qualify as a customer. This addition creates uncertainty on who is considered a customer when other sections reference the term 'Customer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 1 DEFINITIONS 1.1 "Customer" means a person or entity that has acquired, or has indicated its interest in acquiring from Licensee, or from a Redistributor if so specified herein, a non-exclusive and nontransferable Sublicense to use one (1) or more of the Licensed Products, and has maintained an active subscription to support services for a minimum of one year. 1.2 "Documentation" means all visually readable materials published or made available by Licensor during the term of this Agreement for use by Customers in connection with the Licensed Products. 1.3 "Emergency Fix" means a temporary correction of a problem in a Licensed Product reported by Licensee to Licensor that may take the form of a written instruction or magnetic or optical media. 1.4 "Licensed Product" means any copy, or part thereof, of object code of the software products listed on EXHIBIT A attached to this Agreement, as well as any Upgrades or other material distributed to Licensee by Licensor in connection with such software products. 1.5 "Master Copy" means the initial object code copy of each Licensed Product and of any subsequent Upgrades or other derivations distributed to Licensee by Licensor under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition ('maintained an active subscription to support services for a minimum of one year') to the definition of 'Customer'. This creates a contradiction within the contradicted text where the definition of customer is stated again with same the added condition. This satisfies the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 7.9 MODIFICATIONS. Licensee shall not make any modifications to or derivations of the Licensed Products without the prior written consent of Licensor, except in the case of an Emergency Fix. Licensee shall not reverse engineer or otherwise attempt to reproduce the source code of any Licensed Product. In the event that Licensee makes any modification, alteration or enhancement to the Licensed Product or Documentation (including but not limited to an Emergency Fix), such modification, alteration or enhancement, including all intellectual property rights thereto, will be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Licensor. Any suggestions or changes desired by Licensee to the Licensed Product or Documentation shall be made by 8 Licensee in writing to Licensor and, if incorporated into the Licensed Product or Documentation, shall be the property of Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 7.9 MODIFICATIONS. Licensee shall not make any modifications to or derivations of the Licensed Products without the prior written consent of Licensor, except in the case of an Emergency Fix. Licensee shall not reverse engineer or otherwise attempt to reproduce the source code of any Licensed Product. In the event that Licensee makes any modification, alteration or enhancement to the Licensed Product or Documentation (including but not limited to an Emergency Fix), such modification, alteration or enhancement, including all intellectual property rights thereto, will be and remain the joint property of Licensor and Licensee. Any suggestions or changes desired by Licensee to the Licensed Product or Documentation shall be made by 8 Licensee in writing to Licensor and, if incorporated into the Licensed Product or Documentation, shall be the property of Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes the intellectual property ownership of modifications made by the Licensee. Originally, all such modifications became the sole property of the Licensor. The altered version states that these modifications become the joint property of both the Licensor and Licensee. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in the same section that any suggestions incorporated will be the property of licensor, creating an inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 7.9 MODIFICATIONS. Licensee shall not make any modifications to or derivations of the Licensed Products without the prior written consent of Licensor, except in the case of an Emergency Fix. Licensee shall not reverse engineer or otherwise attempt to reproduce the source code of any Licensed Product. In the event that Licensee makes any modification, alteration or enhancement to the Licensed Product or Documentation (including but not limited to an Emergency Fix), such modification, alteration or enhancement, including all intellectual property rights thereto, will be and remain the joint property of Licensor and Licensee. Any suggestions or changes desired by Licensee to the Licensed Product or Documentation shall be made by 8 Licensee in writing to Licensor and, if incorporated into the Licensed Product or Documentation, shall be the property of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the ownership of modifications from the Licensor to joint ownership between Licensor and Licensee. This directly contradicts the subsequent sentence in the same section stating that any suggestions or changes desired by Licensee, if incorporated, shall be the property of Licensor. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the same section regarding intellectual property rights, which fulfills the criteria for an in-text contradiction under the Ambiguities definition.

perturbation.original_text: Section 12.2 PERFORMANCE. For twelve (12) months after delivery of any Licensed Product to a Customer, whether Customer receives such Licensed Product from Licensee or a Redistributor, Licensor warrants that each Licensed Product will perform as described in the applicable Documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 12.2 PERFORMANCE. For six (6) months after delivery of any Licensed Product to a Customer, whether Customer receives such Licensed Product from Licensee or a Redistributor, Licensor warrants that each Licensed Product will perform as described in the applicable Documentation.
perturbation.explanation: This change reduces the warranty period from twelve months to six months. This directly contradicts Section 12.3, which references the 'twelve (12) month warranty period', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the length of the warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 12.3 DUTIES UNDER WARRANTY. If Licensee or any Redistributor or Customer discovers any error in any Licensed Product or discrepancy in any Licensed Product from the Documentation that results in a material loss of performance in the Licensed Product within the twelve (12) month warranty period, then Licensor shall provide Licensee with the correction or method of resolving such error or discrepancy provided that Licensor shall not be responsible for any error or discrepancy caused by failure to use the Licensed Products as specified in the Documentation or any modifications made to any Licensed Product by or on behalf of a party other than Licensor. If such error or discrepancy is not resolved within thirty (30) days after Licensee's written notice to Licensor, then Licensee as its sole remedy may (a) extend the correction period to a date which is agreeable to Licensor and Licensee or (b) return all copies of the Licensed Products to Licensor with a certification by an authorized representative of Licensee that all copies have been returned to Licensor or have been destroyed and that Licensee has not retained any copies thereof and Licensor shall refund to Licensee the amount paid by Licensee to Licensor for such Licensed Products. Licensee shall pay for all services rendered by Licensor in connection with the Licensed Products or Documentation that are not covered or at that time are no longer covered by the warranty described in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the warranty period from twelve months to six months in Section 12.2. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Section 12.3, which explicitly references a 'twelve (12) month warranty period'. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections provide conflicting obligations. The contradiction is clear, direct, and impacts the enforceability of the warranty terms.
